title: pilosella bauhini subsp. cymantha
intro: Pilosella bauhini subsp. cymantha, also known as Alpine Mouse-ear or Cypselium, is a subspecies of the Pilosella genus, a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it beneficial for wound healing, skin conditions, and reducing pain and swelling. The health benefits of Pilosella bauhini subsp. cymantha include reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and promoting overall well-being. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and skin irritations.
